articles.title: Scaffolding Project Completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve
articles.excerpt: A scaffolding project has been completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve to facilitate bridge restoration works. The project involved erecting scaffolding for waterproof encapsulation.
articles.content_html:
A scaffolding project has been completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve, Rochdale. The scaffolding was erected to provide a waterproof encapsulation to enable bridge restoration works.

The project highlights the ongoing efforts to maintain and improve local natural sites, ensuring they remain accessible and safe for visitors.

Local contractors undertook the work, which is expected to enhance the structural integrity of the bridge and prolong its lifespan.

The completion of this project may mean improved access for walkers and nature enthusiasts visiting the reserve.

articles.title: Rochdale Weather Forecast: Sunny Intervals Expected This Week
articles.excerpt: The Met Office has issued a weather forecast for Rochdale, predicting sunny intervals and mild temperatures over the coming days.
articles.content_html:
The Met Office has released the latest weather forecast for Rochdale, indicating a week of mild temperatures and sunny intervals. Today, temperatures are expected to reach a maximum of 21 degrees Celsius, with a minimum of 11 degrees overnight.

On Sunday, the forecast predicts a high of 21 degrees Celsius with sunny intervals and a low of 13 degrees. Rain is unlikely, with less than a 5% chance of precipitation throughout the day.

Monday and Tuesday are expected to see similar conditions, with highs of 23 degrees Celsius and a slight chance of rain in the afternoon. Wind gusts could reach up to 18 mph on Sunday and 15 mph on Monday.

The forecast for the rest of the week shows temperatures gradually decreasing, with highs of 22 degrees on Wednesday and 21 degrees on Thursday, before dropping to 19 degrees on Friday. Residents may wish to take advantage of the sunny weather while it lasts.

articles.title: Rochdale Council Staff Acknowledged for Their Support
articles.excerpt: Three members of the Environmental Management Department at Rochdale Borough Council received public thanks for their assistance during a recent incident.
articles.content_html:
A post on social media has expressed gratitude towards three members of the Environmental Management Department at Rochdale Borough Council. The staff members, named Julian, Mark, and Brett, were praised for their helpfulness during a recent incident.

The post highlighted how the three staff members 'literally came to our rescue' and noted their kindness and support. The message referred to them as 'absolute stars', reflecting the positive impact of their assistance.

This acknowledgment comes as part of ongoing community engagement, where residents are encouraged to recognise the efforts of local council staff in providing essential services.

Rochdale Borough Council continues to promote a culture of appreciation for its employees, aiming to foster strong relationships between the council and the community.

articles.title: Weather Forecast for Castleton: July 16, 2026
articles.excerpt: The weather forecast for Castleton, Rochdale, on July 16, 2026, predicts a mix of sun and clouds with occasional rain showers expected. Temperatures are likely to reach a high of 20 degrees Celsius, while overnight lows may drop to around 12 degrees Celsius.
articles.content_html:
The weather forecast for Castleton, Rochdale, on July 16, 2026, predicts a mix of sun and clouds with occasional rain showers expected.

Temperatures are likely to reach a high of 20 degrees Celsius, while overnight lows may drop to around 12 degrees Celsius.

People should carry umbrellas as rain is expected intermittently throughout the day, particularly in the afternoon.

For those planning travel or outdoor activities, it is advisable to check for updates on weather conditions as they may change.

articles.title: UK Faces Unprecedented Climate Change with Extreme Temperatures
articles.excerpt: The UK is experiencing unprecedented climate change, with extreme temperatures expected to persist in the coming years. Experts warn that the impacts of climate change are increasingly evident across the country.
articles.content_html:
The UK is experiencing unprecedented climate change, with extreme temperatures expected to persist in the coming years. Experts warn that the impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly evident across the country.

Recent reports indicate that the frequency and intensity of heatwaves are likely to increase, posing challenges for public health and infrastructure.

In Rochdale, residents may notice changes in weather patterns, which could affect local services and community activities.

The ongoing climate crisis highlights the need for collective action to address environmental issues and promote sustainability within communities.

articles.title: Twelve Rochdale borough green spaces retain Green Flag Awards
articles.excerpt: Twelve parks and green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received Green Flag Awards for another year.
articles.summary: Rochdale Borough Council has celebrated Green Flag Award success at 12 local green spaces, praising staff and volunteers who maintain the parks throughout the year.
articles.content_html:
Twelve parks and green spaces across Rochdale borough are celebrating another year of Green Flag Award success.

The recognised sites are located across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines.

Rochdale Borough Council described the awards as a significant achievement for the employees and volunteers who work throughout the year to keep the borough’s green spaces welcoming and accessible.

Recognition for well-managed green spaces

The Green Flag Award is an international quality mark for parks and green spaces.

Sites are assessed against standards covering areas such as cleanliness, environmental management, biodiversity, safety, community involvement and the quality of their facilities.

The awards recognise spaces that are well managed and provide benefits for residents, visitors and local wildlife.

Thanks to staff and volunteers

The council praised the teams and community volunteers who contribute to the upkeep of local parks throughout the seasons.

Their work includes maintaining paths, gardens and planted areas, caring for habitats and helping to ensure the spaces remain pleasant places for recreation and relaxation.

“A fantastic achievement for our teams and volunteers who work year-round to create welcoming spaces that offer so many benefits for residents and visitors to our borough.”

Residents are being encouraged to look out for Green Flags flying at their local award-winning park or green space.

This report is based on a public announcement and photographs published by Rochdale Borough Council following the latest Green Flag Award results.
articles.event_location: Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines
